headline: Oil prices fall to around $100 after Trump indicates war could end in weeks
summary: Crude prices fell Wednesday as President Donald Trump signaled a willingness to exit the Iran war even as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

headline: CNBC Daily Open: Markets rally as Trump signals Iran war could end soon
summary: The impacts from the Iran war continue to way on global supply chains as economists raise their expectations for a potential recession in the U.S.

headline: South Korea's Kospi leads rebound in Asia markets as Trump says Iran war could end in weeks
summary: On Tuesday stateside, Trump said the U.S. could leave Iran in "two or three weeks," adding "We leave because there's no reason for us to do this."

raw.headline: South Korea's Kospi leads rebound in Asia markets as Trump says Iran war could end in weeks

headline: Wednesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
summary: The three major averages soared Tuesday on traders' hope that an end could be in sight for the war in Iran. The Dow Industrials jumped more than 1,100 points.

headline: Stocks are higher to start the month as optimism around Iran war ending grows: Live updates
summary: Stocks rose on Wednesday, while oil prices declined to start the month, as hope grew that an end to the U.S.-Iran war was on the horizon.

raw.headline: Stocks are higher to start the month as optimism around Iran war ending grows: Live updates
raw.summary: Stocks rose on Wednesday, while oil prices declined to start the month, as hope grew that an end to the U.S.-Iran war was on the horizon.

headline: Why $4 a gallon gas prices won’t trigger Fed interest rate hikes — and could lead to cuts
summary: While there's still plenty of uncertainty about where rates are headed, Wall Street commentary shifted back to expectations for cuts.

headline: Energy Stocks Set to Beat Broader Market by Biggest Margin Ever
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US energy stocks are on track to top the broader market by the biggest margin ever, as investors rushed to the group amid the Middle East war and a broader rotation away from pricey technology names. The S&P 500 Energy Index is up 39% in the first quarter so far, compared to a 7% decline in the S&P 500 Index. The group has been on a stellar winning streak, rising for 14 straight weeks, far ahead of the previous record from 2007 when they rose for nine weeks. Firms like Exxon Mobil

headline: Trump lashes out at UK and France, telling allies 'the U.S.A. won't be there to help you anymore'

headline: South Korea proposes extra $17 billion budget to cushion energy costs fueled by Iran war
summary: Seoul imports 94% of its energy needs, and almost 72% of its crude oil comes from the Middle East.
